name: Pressure Effects on Gas Reactions
description: Raising pressure increases gas-phase partial pressures and collision frequency, accelerating bimolecular reactions; unimolecular reactions show Lindemann fall-off where the apparent order changes with pressure, and changes in gas moles shift equilibrium.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Gas-phase reaction"]
    N2["Increase total pressure"]
    N3["Partial pressures rise"]
    N4["Collision frequency up"]
    N5{"Molecularity?"}
    N6["Bimolecular: rate rises"]
    N7["Unimolecular (Lindemann)"]
    N8["Fall-off: order shifts"]
    N9["Δn(gas) shifts equilibrium"]
    N10["New rate and position"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 -->|bimolecular| N6
    N6 --> N9
    N5 -->|unimolecular| N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 --> N9
    N9 --> N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff

    style N3 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff

Physical Chemistry, 11th ed. — Reaction Rates and Pressure
